entry.title: Re: Report(s) that show After the Fact (ATF) Purchase Orders
entry.content: Hi Marcus, Please help to clarify what's your purchase order date mean? For the purchasing order, I would like to explain it first, based on the sort key setting with purchase order no. in the GR/IR account, you will get the purchasing order no. in the line item level after posting the invoice verification. Kind Regards, Lucy

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4...
entry.content:
I would try to stay away from using back end ecc user store for authentication and use x.509 SSO whenever possible. Users on the backend ECC still have to have proper authorizations in order for apps to work.

I would focus on letting Identity Provider handling the authentication work and ECC only have to worry about the apps and data once it gets past the authentication.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4...
entry.content: By the way, Elion, it was proven to work fine. I’ve set it up and tried it with different roles and authorization. Authentication process remained the same. access on the back end was changed when users don’t have proper authorization.

entry.title: Minimum desktop and laptop hardware requirements
entry.content:
Could you please advice Minimum desktop and laptop hardware requirements, Thanks 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Minimum desktop and laptop hardware requirements
entry.content:
As the product runs with web browser, simply speaking if web browser is usable, then the computer can use public cloud.

So we can check the minimum requirement for example for Chrome:

Chrome browser system requirements - Chrome Enterprise and Education Help (google.com)

entry.title: Replicating House Bank Data from S4 to S4 - IDOC not getting generated for BAMMAST
entry.content:
House Bank data flow was active from S4 to ECC - Message Type HBHBAMAST. Recently upgrade ECC system to S4, now the data flow from S4 to S4 . As per SAP, two message types to configure i.e. BAMMAST and HBHBAMAST.  HBHBAMAST working as expected for all the options as per screen shot below. BAMMAST does NOT trigger any IDOC with filters(DRFF). Please find the details below.  

Transaction DRFOUT - BAMMAST/HBHBMAST works as expected with options "initialization" and "Manual". 

With "Changes" option  - Works ok with out filters however not consistent; With Filters using DRFF, BAMMAST does NOT trigger any IDOC. 

I would value your insights on this matter.

Thank You for your time.

entry.content:

I have some code experience about more than 4 years.

Its excited to join in！

thank you

Zernan

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 Company Database not Displaying on the SLD
entry.content: hi all just to update for reference i follow sap note 2788059 to re install server tools suggested but issue still the same... i ended up re installing complete SAP and recover backup... finally work... thank you everyone for sharing your knowledge...

entry.title: Re: How to use I_JournalEntryTP in custom RAP BO
entry.content:
Hi,

The example that shows in this blog is one of the ways to use I_JouranlEntryTP.  

You can have any kind of your own logic which responsible to perpare the posting parameter and make sure no error, then you can call 'Post' to finally post general entry.

Please check in the documentation on how use in local program.  If you want to use 'Post' in your own RAP object, then 'Post' action only be allowed in RAP specific methods.

The 'Post' should be used in the no-return-point in your own logic, which means that you should guarantee that there is no error happens during the posting.  Otherwise it will have inconsistent or ST22 dump. 

It is strongly recommended to call 'Validate' action before calling 'Post'.

 Best regards,
Take

entry.title: Re: Third party Sales process in SAP S/4 HANA system
entry.content:
Hi Team ,

This process has been changed in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud - Item category: CB2 and Item category group : CBNA and Scope ( BKD ) .

entry.title: SD downpayment amount should include tax value
entry.content:
hi everyone I used billing plan functionality in sales order process. here is my billing plan as shown below.

 

 

 

 

the FAZ billing amount is net amount. 

When I issue invoice for FAZ down-payment, I need to generate the down-payment request to our customer with a gross amount.  

I checked the note 213567 about down-payment net/gross process, it said only setup the GL master with +B-down payments managed gross. 

I modifed reconciliation account for down-payment, and modifed the Tax category with option +B 

but the generated note accounting document as well as invoice document are both still net amount,not gross amount

can anybody help me on this? how to have the down-payment request to customer with a gross amount NOT net amount? when customer pay, the 30% contract amount is including tax!

entry.title: Re: Need CDS view name to fetch User name based on CB** b...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Could you please have a look at SAP Help Portal - Business User Basic?
CDS View Name is I_BusinessUserBasic, and in the structure part, there are some fields like "PersonFullName" or "UserID".

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Reassign costs and revenues - new app without excel u...
entry.content:
Hi Buddy,

Yes, the upload button is not in the screen of creating page in S4HC 2408, the current solution is to use API to mass process the scenario. Please refer to note https://me.sap.com/notes/3485138/E for details.

Or follow the copy and paste function in note https://me.sap.com/notes/3071023/E

Or CIP can be created to check if the upload function can be released in new version.

Br, Bob

entry.title: Re: Run report showing machine time for a mass list of PN...
entry.content:
Hi there, you could try CEWB with the Current Working Area = SAP_Operation.  Input the required skus, execute, then look for the standard values in the Processing Times tab.

DH

entry.title: Re: Friends helping friends solve the SAP Crossword puzzle
entry.content:
Check out my blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/unboxing-cloud-functions-in-sap-build-apps/ba-p/13562231 where I discuss the cloud functions when they first came out, including this flow function. It is also a common word when running queries or other scripts that sometimes replaces Run. 

It is also a type of privilege in SAP HANA, and in Microsoft's Transact-SQL it is a keyword that runs a command string or stored procedure.

Also, if you get the other answers around it you'll likely start to see the answer in the grid.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4...
entry.content:
Hi ELion,

There are some guides already in the community and SAP document, so I keep this short by assuming you are using the WZ standard edition:

- Follow this for setup and troubleshooting: https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/asjava/3361376259.html 

- You can check this SAP mission, which will give you practical insights: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/3283/3378

as per your requirement, you should set up a principal propagation between BTP and ECC via Cloud Connector and maintain common name attributes according to your requirement. However, logging on to BTP with your ECC credential is impossible. To achieve that, you must configure the authentication via your own IDP: SSO to ECC and SSO to BTP via your custom IDP.

Hope it helps.

entry.title: Re: JIT Processing with the Next-Generation Just-in-Time in S/4HANA - Part 1 -
entry.content: Nice summary! It helps a lot.

entry.title: Re: Smart choices for your UI: When to use sap.ui.mdc
entry.content:
Cool, such a nice sharing.

" In conclusion, sap.ui.mdc offers powerful features for SAPUI5 applications, particularly when it meets your project’s requirements. By assessing the scenarios and implementation approaches you can enhance your application's functionality and user experience while keeping the development effort as low as possible. "

🙂

Regards.

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: Internal behaviour of dump marker and rsync marker in...
entry.content:
Hi Dilip

1. the transactions prior to the dump marker will be skipped and not applied to RDBs, since the data is expected to be replaced by the dump contents. For the very first step in a resync scenario, we will instruct the repserver connections to be in a "resync" mode, ie resume connection to xxx skip to resync marker, see details below: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_REPLICATION_SERVER/62f3e3a6d49342cf9b7d72ee726a48b0/fd0b72adbd1c10148f75e408192572df.html?locale=en-US&q=resync+marker 

and

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_REPLICATION_SERVER/62f3e3a6d49342cf9b7d72ee726a48b0/fd0bedadbd1c1014b1beabd7f2f84de7.html?locale=en-US&q=resync+marker

 

2. yes we'd recommend use resync marker as it's easier to handle.
Check out the difference for using resync marker and dump marker:


2308228 - How to resynchronize multiple MSAs with one Primary Database dump in SRS

2605522 - How to resync target DB with Dump Marker in MSA system? - SRS

 

Cheers

Tony

entry.title: Re: 公有云下，供应商收不到采购邮件
entry.content: 谢谢，已经根据1LQ处理，需要 SAP协助激活 'XX-S4C-OPR-SRV'组件，并让客户设置SAP提供的record在DNS中，后续以客户域发邮件就可以了。

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud - Hide prices on invoice
entry.content:
My Dear Community Friend,

In the past days, I tried to reach different colleagues for your questions, unfortunately, there is no perfect answer for your question.

May I ask whether you had some progress for your issues in between?

If it's not, and it's also criticial for you to achieve the behavior, you may create new entry in the SAP Customer Influence website.

Best Regards,

Sean

entry.title: Re: Team owner - Manage Teams and Responsibilities
entry.content:
Hi Rajesh,

 About your question "Hello Expert, I am also looking for a solution. How to update USR21-IDADTYPE & USR21-GUID?", please follow the https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/c6e6d078ab99452db94ed7b3b7bbcccf/52671191439b11d1896f0000e8322d00.html?locale=en-US&version=202210.latest (via transaction SU01, just create a person user (i.e. a non-technical user).

USR21-IDENTITY_GUID will be auto generated.

For making this user a business user, ideally this should be set using the transaction SU01. The USR21-IDADTYPE must be either '02' (Identity (User Account with BP Person)) or '04' (Identity with Workplace Address).

Hope it clarifies your questions.

Best Regards,

Abinash

entry.title: Re: SAP CLOUD - EDOC_IT_XML_FILE - update description in...
entry.content: My Dear Community Friend, May I ask whether you had any progress for your question? if not, as you also mentioned the sample code is also not working, one potential option would be you create an ticket for this, then the SAP expert may have the chance to check it in your system.

entry.title: Re: Migration platform.appgyver.com/builder for SAP BUILD
entry.content: Hello, I also need to recover my project, I could not do the migration

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4...
entry.content:
Hi,

As other replies have pointed out the recommended approach should be to use authentication service of Cloud Identity (or any SAML/OIDC identity provider) and configure principal propagation to the ECC backend. Technically it is not possible to use ECC WebGUI logon page for BTP cloud applications simply because ECC/ABAP system is not a SAML Identity Provider. BTP applications including WorkZone require SAML/OIDC Identity Provider to authenticate for federated SSO. The SAML configuration on ABAP is for Service Provider only. 

Regards,

Tong

entry.title: Why RISE with SAP is the most secure way to run your ERP
entry.content:
✨Join us to learn about the security processes, innovative solutions and services that are provided by RISE with SAP to help protect business data and operations, support regulatory requirements, and strengthen enterprise resilience.
Discover how RISE with SAP fosters vital collaborations to enhance security and compliance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM support for Oracle databases
entry.content:
Hi Gavin,

Monitoring for Oracle database is currently not supported in SAP Cloud ALM.
The same idea has been raised to our product team in the SAP Influence page: Database monitoring from CALM (e.g. DBACOCKPIT functionality) for any supported DB.

You may vote on the above idea or raise your new idea in SAP Influence for your request.
And here is the guide for the available monitoring content in Health Monitoring.

Best regards,
Peng

entry.title: Re: How to Add Attachment to BP Store Business document
entry.content:
Hello,

After running BP transaction, when you entered you can find menu option there you can able create attachment. Please let me know if you need more help on it.

entry.title: Re: MASS transaction for Dispute Management
entry.content: Can you please help me more: I have around 2000-3000 open disputes. I have to update reasons for it. if I do it manually it takes more time. So there any easy way to make the change.

entry.title: Re: Time Calculation Data Action - From To Period
entry.content:
Hi

The Data type "TEXT" is the type of Property [p/LockedTo] and the "Numeric" data type is not recognized as the Date "yyyymm" or" yyyy.mm.dd" format.  

 

FYI, below is the Supported Date format for Advanced formulas,

  • YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM, YYYY-Q, YYYY, YYYYMMDD, YYYYMM, YYYYQ, YYYY/MM/DD, YYYY/MM, YYYY/Q, YYYY.MM.DD, YYYY.MM, YYYY.Q

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: CIG Mapping For Additional Partner Function in cXML Order Request.
entry.content: if you have a buyer integrator - you can ask for his help. if not, you can log a ticket to support and request a custom xslt mapping to be done in backend

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Flexibility: Adapting UIs for Specific User Roles
entry.content:
Hi @RaminS ,

If applications have activated the "requireAppAuthorizations" property for their cards, only users with access to the specific app will be able to view these cards. However, we suggest reaching out to the 'My Sales Overview' (F2200) application by raising an incident to verify if they can enable this property for their cards.

Thanks.

💫Join Paul and Phil as they explore the latest innovations in ERP, with a focus on SAP's role in driving the future of business technology.
🚀They'll discuss the integration of AI, machine learning, and IoT into ERP systems and how these technologies can transform business operations, improve decision-making, and foster a culture of continuous improvement. You are invited to submit questions for a live Q&A with the speakers.

entry.title: Database restore / Cleaning activity for SAP S4HANA Public Cloud Quality and Customising Server
entry.content:
Dear Team,

we are implementing SAP S4HANA Public Cloud at one of our clients.

1. We want to clean entire data along with all masters from Customizing server or from Quality server keeping all validations, configurations as it is.

2.  Also, we want to restore Production server data along with transactions, masters and configurations to Quality server to replicate Production server on Quality server.

We would like if this activity can be possible, if yes, how?

Thanks,

Nitin

entry.title: CDS view name BP's Assigned to Partner function against supplier
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to fetch the Email address from the Assigned BP for custom Partner functions defined for supplier, i have tried checking I_SupplierPartnerFunc, I_SupplierPartnerFunc_2 CDS views but the 'PersonnelNumber' field against the Partner function is blank.

Could you please help me find the right CDS view to fetch the Assigned BP for the custom partner function maintained for the supplier. please find below screenshot for your reference.

Thank you in Advance.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Scheduled Backup Failures on SAP HANA Tenant Database
entry.content:
We are encountering recurring issues with the scheduled daily data backup for one of our tenant databases,  on our SAP HANA server Our server hosts three tenant databases.

The above said database backup fails once or twice a week, and the failures occur under varying conditions:

  1. Stale PID Issue:
    • Sometimes the error log indicates that the backup process has stale PIDs.
    • Upon checking the PID file (located in /var/log/SAPBusinessOne/BackupService/logs/abc-hana_30015/XXX_LIVE.pid), we find multiple PIDs associated with the same schema.
    • Deleting the older PIDs resolves the issue, and the backup completes successfully after that.
  2. Backup Failure without Stale PID:
    • At other times, the backup fails without the stale PID issue. In these cases, the only solution we’ve found is to stop the HANA services and restart the operating system. Once restarted, the backupserver proceeds without issues.

what is the issue related for this and what actions should we follow to resolve the matter

I am eagerly seeking support from this esteemed community.

entry.title: Re: ZMM_MISSMATCH_PUR
entry.content: ZMM_MISSMATCH_PUR is a custom report, specific to your implementation only.

entry.title: Re: Intra-Company STO Process (Two Step Process) in S/4 HANA
entry.content: Very nice document for end to end config. Just wondering wouldn't setting up pricing procedure for STO is not required here? Please suggest.

entry.title: Re: sap sac planning professional license in Prod
entry.content:
Hi Abdul,

you will need at least one Planning Professional in your productive environment to be able to create, update or delete planning models. You can find the description of what is included in each license type here: Features by License Type for Planning Models | SAP Help Portal

Also you need this Planning Professional for your administration, as this is also not included in the Planning Standard, as described here: Features by License Type for Analytic Models | SAP Help Portal

Apart from that you do not need the Planning Professional in the productive environment and can work in the development environment. Also those developers won't need Planning Standard licenses in your DEV if they have the Planning Professional.

Best regards,

Torben

entry.title: Re: Friends helping friends solve the SAP Crossword puzzle
entry.content: Thank you! Now I get it 😃 I've tried many things but didn't come up with this one

entry.title: Re: Non-archiving solution for GRACAUDITLOG data cleanup in SAP GRC
entry.content:
Dear all!

Thanks Shivendra for the blog about the usage of GRAC_SPM_CLEANUP, it is perfectly describe the usage of it.

Although the above blog is describe a possible use case, please understand that this report is designed to be used in mostly Sandbox systems or any other system where no Productive data in the tables. This report is a simple cleanup report, and nothing more. It is remove the data from the relevant tables without checking the connection and the consistency of them, hence it is not designed to be even a similar solution to archiving. SAP can not take any responsibility of the usage and the consequences of the usage of it. We always suggest to do a backup of your data before you running this report, as it could cause inconsistency if it is not used properly.

GRACAUDITLOG table storing the Audit log entries (SM20) which was collected for a Firefighter Session. If this table is growing rapidly that could also means that you selects and collects many Audit log related entries in your backend. Instead of removing the data we suggest to analyze the collected data and check if every Audit event is really required based on your compliance policy. If the collected data is required, then we suggest to provide adequate database space and archive them after it is not necessary.

Any other case the above post is a very useful to remove the unwanted data.

Best regards, Mate

entry.title: Re: How to use etag for change operations in S/4HANA OData APIs
entry.content: Unfortunately the header etag is not returned in the newer ODATA V4 GET Adapter from SAP. This makes it impossible to do this with the ODATA V4 Adapter.

entry.title: Oil and gas
entry.content:
My job require these info , which courses should I do

  • Strong knowledge of SAP SD and MM
  • Hands-on experience on SAP IS-Oil, TSW, TD Shipment, HPM and Exchanges would be an asset
  • Business and technical experience supporting SAP S/4 focus in pricing
  • Basic knowledge of BTP, ABAP coding and debugging would be an asset
  • SAP related certifications (S4 HANA) or equivalent
  • Support  Manage Supply and Products business process (Product 2 Cash), implemented in SAP S/4, focused primarily in Oil and Gas downstream business processes
  • Manage the SAP function of pricing and billing
  • Support various interfaces with trading partners or in-house external systems
  • Responsible for translating the user’s requirement into a Functional/technical specification on which the new SAP configuration will be based

entry.title: sql server 2008 Active/standby mode
entry.content:
We want to build SQL Server DIGITS database active/standby mode to achieve real-time synchronization of the whole database, without the need for HA, what technology can be achieved here?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: sql server 2008 Active/standby mode
entry.content:
In SQL Server 2008, to achieve real-time synchronization of the whole database in an active/standby configuration without using High Availability (HA) solutions like Always On Availability Groups (which are not available in SQL Server 2008), you can use SQL Server's Database Mirroring feature.

Database Mirroring is a solution for increasing database availability. It maintains two copies of a single database that must reside on different server instances of SQL Server Database Engine. One server instance serves the database to clients (the principal server), while the other instance acts as a hot or warm standby server (the mirror server).

Here's how you can set up Database Mirroring in SQL Server 2008:

  1. Prepare the Principal and Mirror Servers: Ensure that both servers are properly configured, and SQL Server is installed on both. They should be able to communicate with each other over the network.

  2. Backup and Restore the Database: Take a full backup of the principal database and a subsequent log backup. Then, restore these backups on the mirror server WITH NORECOVERY. This leaves the database on the mirror server in a restoring state, ready to synchronize with the principal database.

  3. Configure Security: Database Mirroring can operate in two different modes: with a witness server or without. If you want automatic failover, you'll need a witness server. You'll also need to configure the appropriate endpoints and security, such as certificates or Windows Authentication, for the servers to communicate.

  4. Establish the Mirror: Use SQL Server Management Studio or T-SQL commands to establish the mirroring session. You'll need to specify the principal and mirror server instances and the database to mirror.

  5. Monitor the Mirror: Once established, monitor the mirroring status to ensure that the databases remain synchronized. SQL Server Management Studio provides a Database Mirroring Monitor that you can use to track the status.

  6. Failover: In the event of a failure on the principal server, you can manually failover to the mirror server. If you've configured a witness server and set up automatic failover, this can happen without manual intervention.

Please note that Database Mirroring is deprecated in later versions of SQL Server, and Microsoft recommends using Always On Availability Groups, which were introduced in SQL Server 2012, for new applications. However, for SQL Server 2008, Database Mirroring is a suitable technology for your requirements.

Keep in mind that SQL Server 2008 has reached the end of its extended support lifecycle as of July 9, 2019. It's highly recommended to upgrade to a newer version of SQL Server to benefit from the latest features, improvements, and security updates.

entry.title: How To reverse the Stock UPLOADED UNDER /SCWM/ISU

The stock was Also Posted In Export Sytem, it is urgently needed

Thank you for Your support

Remember it's been 12 days since go live, I am a beginner, the answer has to be simplified

entry.title: Deletion of Instance Synchronization Tool
entry.content:
As previously announced in our 1H 2024 What's New Viewer, the Instance Synchronization tool will no longer be available starting from the 2H 2024 release. We recommend that our customers transition to Configuration Transport Center tool which allows you to view, download, and transport configurations across tenants seamlessly.

For more information about the Configuration Transport Center tool, please visit our Help Portal.

entry.title: Re: 🔵 Best of SAP BW Features in SAP Datasphere

Thanks

Ram

entry.title: External display for IW33
entry.content:
• Good Evening All

I have a question, is it possible to to show a work order number and description from iw33 to an external display in maintenence department,  basically when production makes a PM I would like SAP to wright some data like order number , time , comments ,ext to a html page and then the monitor in maintenence can read that html page.

Kinda new to SAP so don't know if it's possible

 

Thanks for your reply in advance

entry.title: What is planned for the 2411 release of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing?
entry.content:
The 2411 release of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing is planned to be deployed to BTP customer tenants on October 23, 2024.

 

This blog covers the following planned innovations:

  • Pricing service – Price Agreements
  • Pricing service – Manage Stored Documents
  • Pricing service – Graduated Interval Scales Factor
  • Pricing service – Proper Returning of Used Currency Exchange Rates
  • Variant Configuration Service – Change the Source of a Configuration
  • Extension Concept – Pass the Variant Factors to Extension Implementations
  • Administration – Alerts in Case of High Number of Deltas
  • Administration – Rate Limits for Calls to the Administration Service
  • Administration – Improved Variant Table Load for Knowledge Bases
  • Administration – New Fields for the Display of Condition Records

 

It also includes a call to act regarding the following potentially incompatible changes:

  • Administration – Rate Limits for Calls to the Administration Service

 

Please check the updated roadmap and release notes after the release date to see which of these features were delivered as planned.

 

Disclaimer

The information in this presentation is confidential and proprietary to SAP and may not be disclosed without the permission of SAP.
Except for your obligation to protect confidential information, this presentation is not subject to your license agreement or any other service or subscription agreement with SAP. SAP has no obligation to pursue any course of business outlined in this presentation or any related document, or to develop or release any functionality mentioned therein.

This presentation, or any related document and SAP's strategy and possible future developments, products and or platforms directions and functionality are all subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice. The information in this presentation is not a commitment, promise or legal obligation to deliver any material, code or functionality.  This presentation is provided without a warranty of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to, the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement. This presentation is for informational purposes and may not be incorporated into a contract. SAP assumes no responsibility for errors or omissions in this presentation, except if such damages were caused by SAP’s intentional or gross negligence.

All forward-looking statements are subject to various risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from expectations. Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on these forward-looking statements, which speak only as of their dates,
and they should not be relied upon in making purchasing decisions.

 

Pricing service – Price Agreements

SAP CPQ plans to support a process called tier pricing, in which the sales agent negotiates certain price scales with the end customer during the quotation process. The sales agent creates a quote and adds items. The price is determined as defined by the pricing procedure using the Pricing service. The sales agent can then negotiate and overwrite scales per price condition per item, which usually means that a lower price per amount is applied for higher item quantities. Those thresholds, where for a certain higher item quantity a lower price is applied, are called tiers. The scales/tiers maintained in SAP CPQ for a quote item will overrule the prices and scales maintained for the product in SAP S/4HANA.

For that, we will introduce new Pricing service endpoints to create, change, read, and delete so-called price agreements. When the calling application passes a price agreement for an item with the negotiated scales for a certain condition type, the Pricing service will use that data to determine the price instead of using what was maintained in the condition record.

The following call creates a new price agreement for the item {itemId} of the pricing document {documentId}:

POST /api/v1/pricing/documents/{documentId}/items/{itemId}/priceAgreements

 

{
  "priceAgreements": [
    {
      "calculationType": "C",
      "conditionTypeId": "PR00",
      "rate": {
        "unit": "EUR",
        "value": "4.99"
      },
      "rateQuantity": {
        "unit": "PCE",
        "value": 1
      },
      "scale": {
        "levels": [
          {
            "scaleRate": {
              "unit": "EUR",
              "value": "4.99"
            },
            "scaleValue": "1.000"
          },
          {
            "scaleRate": {
              "unit": "EUR",
              "value": "3.99"
            },
            "scaleValue": "10.000"
          },
          {
            "scaleRate": {
              "unit": "EUR",
              "value": "2.99"
            },
            "scaleValue": "20.000"
          }
        ],
        "scaleType": "A",
        "scaleUnit": "PCE"
      },
      "validity": {
        "endDate": "2018-12-31",
        "startDate": "2018-01-01"
      }
    }
  ]
}

 

The example above overwrites the price condition PR00. It establishes two tiers:

Tier 1: From item quantity 1 to 9 pieces: 4,99€ per 1 piece (PCE)
Tier 2: For item quantity 10 and above: 3,99€ per 1 piece (PCE)

For a successful price agreement creation, the endpoint returns a price agreement id.

You can only have one price agreement per condition type per item.

You can use the price agreement id with the GET, PATCH, and DELETE endpoints to read, overwrite, or delete it again.

We will not only support this with document pricing but will also enhance the stateless pricing API analogously to allow passing such price agreements at the item level.

More information will be published in the API Definition and in the Development Guide with the November release.

The planned process in SAP CPQ will end with the acceptance of the quote, which will write the negotiated scales into a new condition table in SAP S/4HANA before the contract in SAP S/4HANA is created. With that, it is ensured that those negotiated prices will be applied to the item in the context of this contract.

 

Pricing Service – Manage Stored Documents

When creating a new pricing document with the endpoint POST /documents, it will be possible to specify the owner or the source of the document. The source application, the source type; for example, quote, and the source id; for example, the quote’s transaction number, can be passed and stored with the pricing document.

Example call:

POST /documents

 

{
  "documentCurrencyUnit": "EUR",
  "exchangeRateDate": "2024-09-17",
  "exchangeRateType": "M",
  "localCurrencyUnit": "USD",
  "pricingProcedureId": "ZPRC03",
  "source": {
    "application": "cpq",
    "type": "quote"
    "id": "49709821",
  }
}

 

The source of a pricing document can be specified in the same way when creating a new document as a copy through endpoint POST /documents/{documentId}/copy.

Action:

  • It is recommended to specify the source of the pricing document, so that it can later be used to identify the owner of the document and to judge if it can be deleted again.

The new endpoint GET /documents will be introduced to return a list of stored pricing documents. The API will offer different filter capabilities, for example, filtering based on the source of the document.

A stored pricing document can be read with endpoint GET /documents/{documentId}.

A stored document can be deleted with endpoint DELETE /documents/{documentId}.

More information will be published in the API Definition and the Development Guide with the November release.

 

Pricing service – Graduated Interval Scales Factor

When doing Pricing with Graduated Scales, a factor per scale level is calculated and applied to calculate the condition value from the condition rate. This factor used to overwrite the value of the duration factor in the response field conditions-durationFactor. This loss of information will be corrected by returning the factor separately in the response field conditions-graduatedScaleFactor.

More information will be published in the API Definition and the Development Guide with the November release.

 

Pricing service – Proper Returning of Used Currency Exchange Rates

Condition rates are either converted directly from the condition record currency to the document currency; or they are converted through triangular conversion; first, from the condition record currency to the local currency and then to the document currency. The condition value is always returned in the document currency.

With a request parameter for stateless pricing and document pricing, it is possible to get the used currency conversion rates returned. This allows the calling application to do its own calculations in the same way.

In the currency conversion response: items->currencyConversions->localCurrencyToDocumentCurrency, the returned exchange rate used to be for converting document currency to the local currency instead of the local currency to the document currency. This issue will be corrected. 

 

Variant Configuration Service – Change the Source of a Configuration

When creating a new configuration with the endpoint POST /configurations, the owner or the source of the configuration can be specified. The source application, the source type; for example, quote item, and the source id; for example, the item number 10, can be passed and are stored with the configuration.

The source information can be used as filter criteria in the APIs to manage stored configurations.

With the November release, the enhanced endpoint PATCH /configurations will allow overwriting the source of a configuration in addition to the context:

No partial updates are possible, it is required to provide all the source information: application, type, and id.

Use case: In SAP Commerce Cloud, a user first chooses a product from the product catalog and starts the configuration. Then, the configuration is added to the cart. Finally, the cart is converted into an order. During that process the owner of the configuration changes from the product to the cart item, and finally, to the order item.

More information will be published in the API Definition and in the Development Guide with the November release.

 

Extension Concept – Pass the Variant Factors to Extension Implementations

Variant condition keys are passed to pricing extension implementations using the corresponding pricing attribute, but the variant factors returned by the Variant Configuration service with each key are currently missing.

Remote and local pricing extension interfaces will be enhanced with the optional item input structure variantKeyFactors, allowing the pricing engine to pass the list of all variant keys with the pricing factors when calling a custom pricing routine:

More information will be published in the API Definition and the Extension Guide with the November release.

 

Administration – Alerts in Case of High Number of Deltas

When there is a very high number of deltas that is being replicated, you might want to get notified to check if it would be faster to do a full reload as the performance of the reload is much better compared to that of the delta replication.

The Alert Notification service and SAP Cloud ALM will be used to send notifications about such big numbers of pending deltas.

It will be possible to overwrite the threshold of pending deltas that will trigger the alert:

 

Only applicable for replications based on SAP HANA smart data integration. 

More information will be published in the Administration Guide with the November release.

 

Administration – Rate Limits for Calls to the Administration Service

A rate limit of 5 concurrent API calls per tenant and per server instance will be introduced. The Administration service will run with at least two server instances per region.

The following error message will be returned if the rate limit is reached:
HTTP429 Error: Too many concurrent calls

Please try to reduce the number of concurrent calls and try again.

Additionally, the following limits will apply too:

  • Up to 20 extension archives can be uploaded
  • The last upload must be older than 5 minutes
  • The maximum archive file size is 20 MB
  • There shall be less than 3000 files in an archive
  • A file compressed in the archive must not exceed 30 MB
  • The uncompressed size of all files of the archive must not exceed 100 MB 

More information will be published in the API Definition and the Development Guide with the November release.

 

Administration – Improved variant table load for knowledge bases

The replication mechanism will be improved for variant tables of knowledge bases when reloading: Only variant tables of changed knowledge bases will be loaded.

There will be a new option to enforce a full reload, for example, after a system refresh:

More information will be published in the Administration Guide with the November release.

 

Administration – New Fields for the Display of Condition Records

New fields will be added for the display of condition records:

  • Deletion indicator (KONP-LOEVM_KO): Helps to understand why a condition record is skipped.
  • Exclusion indicator (KONP-KZNEP): Helps to understand why a condition type was skipped.

More information will be published in the Administration Guide with the November release.

 

 

With that, you have a good overview of what innovations are planned for the November release. Please check the updated roadmap and release notes after the release date to see which of these features were delivered as planned.

entry.title: Re: Migration of Asset values in mid-year migration with...
entry.content: Because the excel template doesnt have a ttype (e.g. 07000, 07203) for APC values in current year.

entry.title: BODS Connectivity with Anaplan Data Hub
entry.content:
Hi

We have a requirement to Extract and load data to Anaplan Data Hub, Can we use BODS to extract/load the data directly using Anaplan API's, or do we need any other integrators in between?

entry.title: Re: Configuring Guided Steps in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content: Is it possible to use guided POD for order specific routing ?
